title: A deep learning model for predicting selected organic molecular spectra
core_conclusion: DetaNet, an E(3)-equivariant deep learning model with a self-attention mechanism, predicts four types of organic molecular spectra (IR, Raman, UV-Vis, and 1H/13C NMR) at quantum-chemical accuracy, achieving over 99% accuracy for IR and Raman and over 92% for UV-Vis on the 130,000-molecule QM9 dataset, while running three to five orders of magnitude faster than conventional DFT calculations.
why_it_matters: Enables real-time, high-throughput spectral identification; unifies four spectroscopy modalities under one equivariant architecture; novel Hessian decomposition solves a key bottleneck; transfers to PubChem molecules and distorted geometries; releases QM9S (133,885 molecules) as an open community resource.
why_nature: Broad cross-disciplinary impact across chemistry, materials and pharma; genuine methodological advance in equivariant tensor learning; measurable order-of-magnitude improvements over prior art; large-scale open dataset contribution on a par with QM9.
added_value_to_my_research: Contrastive pre-training on molecular 3D geometry -> self-supervised spatial encoder for GeoAI (paired geospatial snapshots as positives)
Conditional environment input strategy -> policy-conditioned spatial accessibility predictor for what-if planning queries
Forward-model-as-oracle pattern -> differentiable facility-location oracle inside evolutionary/RL optimization loop for EV charging or healthcare access
Equivariant tensor architecture for different-order properties -> equivariant GNN for directional/tensorial spatiotemporal flow prediction on urban graphs
Multi-fidelity two-stage pre-train + fine-tune strategy -> fusion of mobile-phone (low-fidelity) with survey-grade (high-fidelity) mobility data for public health spatial estimates

related.title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks
related.relation: contrastive self-supervised pre-training for molecular graph representations, complementing DetaNet's supervised equivariant approach

related.title: Accurately predicting molecular spectra with deep learning
related.relation: companion paper on deep learning for molecular spectra published in the same journal issue, providing direct methodological comparison

related.title: Deep contrastive learning of molecular conformation for efficient property prediction
related.relation: conformation-aware contrastive learning for molecular property prediction, extends DetaNet's generalization to conformation space

related.title: Approaching coupled-cluster accuracy for molecular electronic structures with multi-task learning
related.relation: multi-task learning to exceed DFT accuracy, directly extending DetaNet's DFT-benchmark ceiling via higher-fidelity training targets

related.title: Unified deep learning framework for many-body quantum chemistry via Green's functions
related.relation: unified DL framework for quantum many-body chemistry, sharing DetaNet's vision of a single model covering multiple quantum-chemical properties

related.title: Overcoming the barrier of orbital-free density functional theory for molecular systems using deep learning
related.relation: deep learning replacement for computationally expensive DFT components, analogous to DetaNet replacing DFT spectral simulations

figures.caption: DetaNet-predicted IR and Raman spectra vs DFT reference and experimental data; includes Hessian, dipole and polarizability derivative regression plots for cyclohexanone, 2-methylpyrazine and caffeine
Performance on UV-Vis (absorption strength error vs. wavelength) and NMR (1H MAE 0.054 ppm, 13C MAE 0.52 ppm) spectra; t-SNE visualization of hydrogen chemical environments
